behavior_summary: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit is a short process that runs when the cobweb object receives a hit and immediately removes the object instance from the world.
The process does not manipulate global flags, present dialogue, or perform branching; its visible effect is cleanup/destruction of the cobweb.
Two parameters (uword, word) are present on entry but unused in the shown code; they likely carry attacker/source metadata or damage type but are not referenced here.
notable_control_flow: Entry to U8P_COBWEB::gotHit is the only observable event; control flow is linear and consists of a single external call to Item::destroy(this).
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or flag reads/writes in the provided function.
After calling Item::destroy(this) the process ends (implicit cleanup).
open_questions: What concrete game data do the parameters 'uword' and 'word' carry for U8P_COBWEB::gotHit (attacker ID, hit type, damage)?
Does destruction trigger any implicit effects elsewhere (drop items, play sound/animation) not visible in this snippet?
Is U8P_COBWEB::gotHit invoked for all damage types or only specific interactions (player attacks vs. environment)?

main_function.name: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle the object being struck/hit by removing the cobweb instance from the game world (one-hit removal).
entry_conditions: Process starts when the cobweb object receives a hit event (U8P_COBWEB::gotHit is invoked by the engine on damage interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit handler that destroys the cobweb instance

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to remove/destroy an item instance

external_calls.purpose: Delete/remove the cobweb item instance (passed as 'this') from the game world

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit is a short process that runs when the cobweb object receives a hit and immediately removes the object instance from the world.
